best_edges.evidence: pullback_long is the largest contributor: 33 trades, $2566.4 PnL, $239.9/hour, 100% win rate, no slow >=12h trades. Worst MAE is meaningful at -403.2, but the profile is still the core portfolio edge.

best_edges.edge: Fast charm-pin pullback scalps despite hidden tail
best_edges.evidence: charm_pin_pullback_long produced 38 trades and $1497.9 with median hold under 5 minutes and average MAE only -68.1, but owns the global -513.4 MAE. This is an edge requiring surgical tail isolation, not broad deactivation.

breakout_long_1dte contributed 20 trades and $1310.2 when 0DTE was unavailable. Prior direct tightening of 1DTE quality gates repeatedly reduced score and net profit, so the existing breakout fallback should be preserved.

charm_wall_breakaway_long has only 3 trades in latest run but $446.2 PnL, $462.1/hour, median MFE 146.2, and no slow >=12h trades. Prior admission-geometry follow-through improved objective, while exit compression hurt.
Extended VWAP winners include 19 protected entries, $736.1 PnL, median MAE -15.3, and median hold 2.27 minutes. Blunt VWAP-extension caps would damage a real fast scalp source.

broken_edges.evidence: Worst trade: 2026-06-26 12:36 UTC, $42.5 PnL but -513.4 MAE and 80-minute hold. It missed the enabled extended-VWAP rejection veto by only the max VWAP distance boundary: actual 26.69 versus current 26.25.
broken_edges.problem: Charm-pin tail is path-dependent and not cleanly separable by one broad static threshold.

broken_edges.evidence: run_038 to run_041 charm-wall realization trim reduced score by -143.7 and net profit by -$146.7 while not improving worst MAE. The profile likely needs convex target room.
broken_edges.problem: Charm-wall exit compression reduces edge more than it reduces risk.

broken_edges.evidence: 1DTE local quality tightening and batch 1DTE tightening both hurt objective and net profit, including -$558.5 and -$317.1 net profit deltas in prior memory.
broken_edges.problem: Directly tightening breakout_long_1dte quality gates cannibalizes profitable fallback trades without reducing portfolio tail enough.

broken_edges.evidence: STALE_0DTE fallback has 5 trades and $162.8, but breakout_long_no_0dte stale subset has low $40.5/hour and -250.25 worst MAE. EXPIRED_0DTE has only one strong winner, so pooled no-0DTE changes risk overfitting.
broken_edges.problem: No-0DTE fallback state is too sparse and mixed for broad pruning.

broken_edges.evidence: breakout_long, wall_reclaim_long, wall_breakout_retest_long, late_day_pin_fade, negative_gamma_momentum_long, and trend_day_pullback_long produced no trades while disabled or inactive in the current evidence set.
broken_edges.problem: Dormant or disabled active-router profiles have no current edge evidence and should not be optimized unless used as explicit discovery splits.

market_regime_read: Short, high-win-rate, take-profit-only sample over about 12.3 calendar days. Realized losses and slow >=12h trades are absent, so the true risk is hidden MAE, long capital tie-up, and path dependency. Current leader remains run_029 at $7149.6 net profit, 107 trades, 0.4% drawdown, -513.4 worst MAE, and $200.5/hour. Latest run_041 added trades and slightly improved PnL/hour, but gave up $146.7 net profit and score versus the leader. The risk-reducer plan should therefore focus on preserving run_029-style throughput while targeting the same -513.4 charm-pin tail through near-miss veto boundaries and profile-aware splits.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Use early post-entry progress to distinguish delayed recovery tails from fast extended-VWAP winners.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Could overfit one or two delayed winners and incorrectly cut high-extension scalps that recover quickly.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Charm Pin Early-Progress Tail Classifier
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Intratrade path fields: first +1.5/+2.0 point time, MFE-before-MAE, first 15m MFE/MAE, profile, ES-VWAP extension, rolling-high distance.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: charm_pin_pullback_long high-extension or recent-high-rejection entries that fail to show early positive progress.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Reduce charm_pin_pullback_long worst MAE from -513.4 toward <= -403 while retaining >=90% charm-pin PnL, >=95% protected extended-VWAP winners, total net profit >=98% of run_029, and slow_12h = 0.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Separate 1DTE pullback/reclaim states from breakout fallback without damaging existing breakout_long_1dte behavior.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Router overlap may cannibalize the profitable 20-trade breakout_long_1dte baseline or the companion may be unreachable.
new_strategy_ideas.name: 1DTE Pullback Companion Profile
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: VALID_1DTE_ONLY trades split by ES reclaim/pullback, structure score, VWAP distance, RSI, ADX, and option-map state.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: 0DTE unavailable, 1DTE map valid, and ES state resembles pullback/reclaim rather than clean breakout continuation.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL >= $1310, 1DTE average MAE improves versus -123.9, total net profit >=98% of run_029, and score flat or better.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Use dormant pin-avoid concepts to split charm-pin/charm-wall tail states through existing router mechanics.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: Activation may be unreachable or may cannibalize profitable charm_pin_pullback_long entries without improving MAE.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Charm Wall Pin-Avoid Dormant Split Probe
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Router attribution, profile standalone PnL, charm-pin/charm-wall combined MAE, protected fast-winner retention.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: VALID_0DTE charm/pin context near wall or pin-avoid behavior where existing charm_pin_pullback_long carries high hidden MAE.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Activated dormant profile has positive standalone PnL or lowers combined charm/pin worst MAE while total net profit remains >=98% of run_029 and protected fast-winner retention remains >=95%.

new_strategy_ideas.edge: Classify pullback_long deep-heat entries separately from core pullback continuation winners.
new_strategy_ideas.failure_mode: May block profitable low-quality pullbacks because pullback_long’s edge tolerates weak setup scores.
new_strategy_ideas.name: Pullback Deep-Heat Companion Classifier
new_strategy_ideas.required_data: Pullback path stats, setup score, structure score, higher-low/high distances, early MFE, MAE timing, and option-state context.
new_strategy_ideas.trigger: pullback_long entries with weak initial progress, poor structure, or delayed recovery behavior.
new_strategy_ideas.validation_metric: Retain >=90% pullback_long PnL while improving pullback worst MAE from -403.2 and keeping total PnL/hour flat or better.

next_experiments.config_bias: Nudge only the enabled extended-VWAP rejection near-miss family. Prioritize the exact boundary that missed the global worst charm-pin tail, and validate against protected extended-VWAP winner UTCS before promotion.
next_experiments.goal: Reduce global worst MAE without broad throughput loss.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The worst charm-pin tail is already inside the intended rejection signature except for a narrow max-distance boundary, so a micro-nudge can catch it while preserving most fast extension winners.
next_experiments.name: Extended-VWAP Near-Miss Tail Repair
next_experiments.primary_metric: Worst MAE, charm_pin_pullback_long PnL retention, protected extended-VWAP winner retention, total score.
next_experiments.risk: The same extension regime produces clean fast winners; too much broadening can remove the edge.

next_experiments.config_bias: Keep charm_pin_pullback_long enabled and avoid setup-quality floors. Test only profile-aware tail boxes combining high extension, pullback-from-high, rolling-high distance, weak RSI/ADX warning state, and stalled VWAP acceleration.
next_experiments.goal: Lower charm-pin tail severity while preserving its fast scalp edge.
next_experiments.hypothesis: Bad charm-pin MAE is concentrated in high-extension/rejection states with delayed recovery, but single setup or VWAP thresholds are too blunt.
next_experiments.name: Charm Pin High-Extension Tail Box Holdout
next_experiments.primary_metric: charm_pin_pullback_long worst MAE <= -403 target, >=90% charm-pin PnL retention, total net profit >=98% of run_029.
next_experiments.risk: Static boxes may still remove delayed profitable recoveries and protected fast winners.

next_experiments.config_bias: Do not compress charm-wall exits. If testing charm_wall_breakaway_long, use only admission geometry around supported wall breakaway and require-breakout continuity; treat it as a small-sample holdout.
next_experiments.goal: Preserve the high-efficiency charm-wall edge while avoiding added tail.
next_experiments.hypothesis: Prior successful charm-wall changes came from admission geometry, while latest exit compression hurt score and net profit.
next_experiments.name: Charm Wall Admission Geometry Reversion Study
next_experiments.primary_metric: charm_wall_breakaway_long PnL/hour >=225, total net profit >=98% of run_029, global worst MAE no worse than -513.4.
next_experiments.risk: Only 3 latest trades; small changes can appear large and may cannibalize existing convex winners.

next_experiments.config_bias: Keep breakout_long_1dte baseline thresholds stable. If legal, study a companion 1DTE pullback split rather than tightening the existing breakout profile.
next_experiments.goal: Reduce 1DTE average/worst MAE without losing the profitable fallback edge.
next_experiments.hypothesis: 1DTE direct tightening has failed repeatedly, so the better research direction is router split attribution, not stricter breakout gates.
next_experiments.name: 1DTE Companion Split Validation
next_experiments.primary_metric: Combined 1DTE PnL, 1DTE average MAE, breakout_long_1dte cannibalization, total score.
next_experiments.risk: Companion routing may be unreachable or may steal good breakout entries.

next_experiments.config_bias: Isolate STALE_0DTE behavior if possible. Do not change VALID_0DTE routing, and do not remove the single EXPIRED_0DTE winner unless state-specific targeting is available.
next_experiments.goal: Improve no-0DTE fallback efficiency and reduce stale fallback heat.
next_experiments.hypothesis: STALE_0DTE fallback has low PnL/hour and moderate MAE, but the data-state sample is sparse and mixed with a strong expired winner.
next_experiments.name: No-0DTE Fallback State Holdout
next_experiments.primary_metric: Fallback PnL/hour, fallback worst MAE, unchanged VALID_0DTE PnL/trade count, total net profit >=98% of run_029.
next_experiments.risk: Very small sample; broad fallback pruning can remove useful insurance without measurable risk reduction.

next_experiments.config_bias: Run exactly one dormant valid-0DTE pin-avoid activation as an attribution experiment only, with minimal local tuning and strict retention checks.
next_experiments.goal: Discover whether an existing dormant pin-avoid concept can absorb tail-heavy charm/pin entries.
next_experiments.hypothesis: The current charm-pin edge is profitable but tail-heavy; a dormant split may separate avoidable pin/charm-wall risk without changing core profile behavior.
next_experiments.name: Dormant Pin-Avoid Router Split Probe
next_experiments.primary_metric: Activated profile standalone PnL, combined charm-pin/charm-wall worst MAE, protected fast-winner retention, total score.
next_experiments.risk: Dormant profile may be inactive in practice or may cannibalize high-quality charm_pin_pullback_long trades.

research_notes: The strongest instruction for the optimizer is restraint. The leader already has low realized drawdown and no slow trades; the problem is hidden MAE in otherwise profitable trades. Broad filters such as setup-quality floors, ES-VWAP caps, or direct 1DTE tightening have either unacceptable PnL loss or failed historically. The next risk-reducer run should target narrow near-miss veto boundaries and profile-aware tail boxes, with explicit protected-winner retention. Latest run_041 is informative but not better than run_029: it added trades and improved trade Sharpe, but lost score and net profit. Use run_029 as the promotion baseline.
risk_controls: Require total net profit >=98% of run_029 before promoting any risk-reducer experiment.
Require completed trades to remain within 10% of leader unless worst MAE improves materially below -403.
Require protected extended-VWAP winner retention >=95% for any charm-pin or VWAP-related entry filter.
Do not use broad setup-quality floors; the setup floor autopsy retained only 42.4% PnL despite improving worst MAE.
Do not repeat charm-wall exit compression; preserve convex target behavior.
Do not directly tighten breakout_long_1dte again; prior experiments hurt objective and net profit.
Treat no-0DTE fallback changes as state-specific holdouts because STALE and EXPIRED samples are too small and behave differently.
